LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CODE FOR AMERICA'S PETITION FOR ORDER AUTHORIZING PRESERVE [SIC] EVIDENCE AND TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY, FOR WHICH NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN RECEIVED, IS GRANTED, PROVIDED THAT PETITIONER PROVIDES A PROPOSED ORDER THAT SATISFIES THE REQUIREMENTS OF SUBDIVISION (B) OF SECTION 2035.050 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, INCLUDING IDENTIFYING THE SPECIFIC DISCOVERY METHOD FOR WHICH AUTHORIZATION IS SOUGHT AND, WITH REASONABLE PARTICULARITY, THE DOCUMENTS TO BE SOUGHT OR WITNESSES TO BE DEPOSED UNDER SUCH AUTHORIZATION. THE COURT UNDERSTANDS THE PETITION TO BE LIMITED TO DOCUMENT INSPECTION DEMANDS. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURTS RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition For Order Authorizing Preserve Evidence And To Perpetuate Testimony
Off Calendar Sep-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER CODE FOR AMERICA LABS, INC. Notice Of Petition And Petition For Order Authorizing Preserve Evidence And To Perpetuate Testimony

Petitioner Code for America's Petition for Order Authorizing Preserve [sic] Evidence and to Perpetuate Testimony, for which no opposition has been received, is GRANTED, PROVIDED THAT Petitioner provides a proposed order that satisfies the requirements of subdivision (b) of section 2035.050 of the Code of Civil Procedure, including identifying the specific discovery method for which authorization is sought and, with reasonable particularity, the documents to be sought or witnesses to be deposed under such authorization. The court understands the petition to be limited to document inspection demands.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
